perturbation.original_text: 11. REMEDIES 11.1 INDEMNIFICATION. Reseller and MediaNet Group Technologies shall indemnify and hold harmless each other, and their respective directors, officers, employees, and agents, from and against all claims, losses, damages and expenses (including reasonable attorney's fees) resulting from the breach of any agreement, representation or warranty set forth herein; provided the indemnified party provides the indemnifying party with (i) prompt written notice of such claim or action, (ii) sole control and authority over the defense or settlement of such claim or action and (iii) proper and full information and reasonable assistance to defend and/or settle any such claim or action.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. REMEDIES 11.1 INDEMNIFICATION. Reseller and MediaNet Group Technologies shall indemnify and hold harmless each other from and against all claims, losses, damages and expenses resulting from the breach of any agreement, representation or warranty set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'their respective directors, officers, employees, and agents' and the conditions for indemnification, the contract reduces the scope of protection, potentially violating laws that mandate protection for these parties in agency agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Duty of Indemnification to Agent's Employees under Pennsylvania Law
perturbation.law_explanation: Pennsylvania law requires employers to provide a safe work environment. Resellers’ employees are acting as agents for MediaNet, therefore are to be protected. This modification weakens the indemnification clause, reducing the protection for employees of Reseller acting as agents of MediaNet Group Technologies, potentially violating this duty of care.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 42 Pennsylvania Consolidated Statutes § 8332.6 (2022) - Antidrug and town-watch volunteer civil immunity :: 2022 Pennsylvania Consolidated Statutes :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Penn. Cons. Stat. 2023 Penn. Cons. Stat. 2022 Penn. Cons. Stat. (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Pennsylvania Consolidated & Unconsolidated Statutes Title 42 - JUDICIARY AND JUDICIAL PROCEDURE Chapter 83 - Particular Rights and Immunities Section 8332.6 - Antidrug and town-watch volunteer civil immunity Universal Citation: 42 PA Cons Stat § 8332.6 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next § 8332.6. Antidrug and town-watch volunteer civil immunity. (a) General rule.-- An antidrug or town-watch volunteer who acts in good faith and within the scope of the volunteer's role with an antidrug or crime prevention volunteer organization or government agency shall be immune from civil liability for damage caused by acts or omissions unless all of the following apply: (1) The conduct of the volunteer falls substantially below the standards generally practiced and accepted in like circumstances by similar persons rendering such services. (2) It is shown that the volunteer performed an act or failed to perform an act which the volunteer was under a recognized duty to another to perform, knowing or having reason to know that such act or omission created a substantial risk or actual harm to the person or property of another. It is insufficient to impose liability under this paragraph to establish only that the conduct of the volunteer fell below ordinary standards of care. (b) Definitions.-- As used in this section, the following words and phrases shall have the meanings given to them in this subsection: "Antidrug or town-watch volunteer." A person performing services for an antidrug or town-watch volunteer organization o
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'their respective directors, officers, employees, and agents' from the indemnification clause. The Perturbation Explanation states that this reduces the scope of protection. The Law Explanation suggests this may violate the duty of care to protect employees of Reseller acting as agents of MediaNet Group Technologies under Pennsylvania law. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 is empty, Scraped Law Snippet 2, 42 Pennsylvania Consolidated Statutes § 8332.6 (2022), discusses immunity from civil liability for volunteers but also mentions standards of care and recognized duties, implying a legal expectation of protection. The omission of specific parties weakens the protection offered, creating a contradiction where the contract no longer explicitly extends indemnification to these individuals, potentially conflicting with the implied duty of care under Pennsylvania law.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for a period of 1 year after the Effective Date, unless earlier terminated as set forth herein (the "TERM"). This Agreement shall renew for successive 1-year periods, after the initial 1 Year Term, if agreed by both parties in writing within 30 days of license expiration. Either party may terminate the Agreement on 60-days written notice during a renewed term. However, in no event shall termination of this Agreement by MediaNet Group Technologies relieve it of the obligation to remit payment to Reseller for sales or Portals, Hosting Services or other products and services to or through Buyers contracted by Reseller prior to such termination. The obligation to remit payment cease 30 days after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for a period of 1 year after the Effective Date, unless earlier terminated as set forth herein (the "TERM"). This Agreement shall renew for successive 1-year periods, after the initial 1 Year Term, if agreed by both parties in writing within 30 days of license expiration. Either party may terminate the Agreement on 60-days written notice during a renewed term. However, in no event shall termination of this Agreement by MediaNet Group Technologies relieve it of the obligation to remit payment to Reseller for sales or Portals, Hosting Services or other products and services to or through Buyers contracted by Reseller prior to such termination. The obligation to remit payment will cease immediately after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change from '30 days after termination' to 'immediately after termination' removes the reseller's right to receive earned commissions for a reasonable period post-termination, potentially violating Florida Statutes regarding payment of commissions.
perturbation.law_explanation: Chapter 545 of the Florida Statutes states that there is protection for sales representatives. The change contradicts the law by possibly withholding earned commission.

perturbation.justification: The original contract stipulates that payment obligations cease 30 days after termination, whereas the changed contract states they cease immediately. The perturbation explanation states that this change removes the reseller's right to receive earned commissions for a reasonable period post-termination, potentially violating Florida Statutes regarding payment of commissions. While the provided statute snippet only points to the existence of Chapter 545 of the Florida Statutes, and does not define any specific requirements, the Law Explanation provides a reason why the change contradicts the law by possibly withholding earned commission. The Omissions Legal Contradiction definition states that a contradiction exists when a contract omits a legally mandated consumer protection, making it non-compliant.

perturbation.original_text: 13.3 GOVERNING LAW. The laws of the State of Florida shall govern this Agreement, without reference to conflicts of law provisions.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.3 GOVERNING LAW. The laws of the State of Nevada shall govern this Agreement, without reference to conflicts of law provisions.
perturbation.explanation: By switching the state to Nevada, and then removing the clause regarding attorneys' fees, the contract increases legal risk because Nevada Revised Statutes 18.010 allows for attorneys' fees in some contract cases, which is omitted here.
perturbation.law_explanation: NRS 18.010 allows for attorney fee awards to the prevailing party in certain contract actions if the other party's claim or defense was brought without reasonable ground or to harass the prevailing party. By failing to acknowledge it, it might mislead parties to think it is not applicable.

perturbation.justification: The change in governing law from Florida to Nevada, coupled with the omission of a clause addressing attorneys' fees, creates a legal contradiction. Nevada Revised Statutes (NRS) 18.010, as indicated in Scraped Law Snippet 1, allows for attorney fee awards to the prevailing party in certain contract actions under specific conditions. By selecting Nevada law without acknowledging this potential for attorney's fees, the modified contract omits a relevant legal consideration that could materially affect the parties' rights and obligations. This omission directly contradicts the consumer protection that Nevada law provides, as explained in the Perturbation and Law Explanations, thus fitting the definition of an Omission Legal Contradiction.
